mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter expansion rate) directly signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings capacity. Positive GDP surprises relative to trend produce positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as equity valuations expand on improved growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 vintage 23542.349 (revised 23586.542) indicates ongoing demand trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate decline signals labor market tightening and wage pressure, which reduces corporate profit margins and increases discount rates. A sustained drop in UNRATE below 4.0% triggers equity market repricing downward over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate Fed tightening response.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite UNRATE < 4.0%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and 5-day forward returns exceeds -0.02 in magnitude less than 30% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate level acts as a persistent drag on equity valuations through the discount rate channel. When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital for equities rises, compressing forward multiples. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts negative 60-day forward returns under high-rate regimes.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward SPX returns when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% do not differ significantly from baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day returns is not negative with |IC| > 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) reflects real supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing health. Sustained INDPRO growth above trend signals robust productive capacity and supports corporate profitability, driving positive 20-day equity returns. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal independent of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or months with INDPRO growth > 0.5% are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength and housing cycle momentum. When both RSAFS growth and HOUST exceed their 12-month moving averages simultaneously, this interaction indicates broad-based demand acceleration that produces positive 20-day SPX returns. The mechanism requires both signals to be active; either alone is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their respective 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST exceed their 12-month MAs, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in fewer than 58% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market momentum. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100k/month while UNRATE rises above 4.2%, this interaction signals labor market deterioration that predicts negative 60-day SPX returns as investors anticipate earnings downgrades from weakening consumer spending.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in fewer than 52% of instances, or the interaction term does not improve model fit (likelihood ratio test p > 0.10) over single-variable models.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate jointly determine real monetary policy stance. When CPI growth accelerates above 3.5% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0%, monetary policy is accommodative in real terms, supporting equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns only when both conditions hold simultaneously.
regime_hint: moderate inflation with accommodative real rates
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in fewer than 56% of instances, or the interaction coefficient reverses sign or becomes insignificant (p > 0.05) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits regime-dependent predictive power for equity returns. In regimes where INDPRO volatility (measured as 3-month rolling standard deviation) exceeds 1.5%, production shocks become material drivers of earnings uncertainty, producing negative 5-day forward returns. In low-volatility regimes, INDPRO trends are less predictive.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO recent revision from 104.2062 to 101.0993 indicates elevated data volatility
regime_hint: high supply-side uncertainty or supply shocks
failure_condition: When INDPRO volatility exceeds 1.5%, 5-day forward SPX returns are negative in fewer than 48% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO level and 5-day returns is positive (contradicting the negative direction prediction) in more than 40% of rolling windows.
